title: Latest construction update for STEAM School in Syracuse reveals plan to create bus loop
summary: The STEAM High School construction project has advanced with city leaders requesting permanent closure of South Warren Street in front of the school to create a dedicated bus loop. The Syracuse Common Council will review the proposal, which requires State Department of Transportation input. The school is on track to open in September 2025 with 250 ninth-grade students.

entity_refs.name: New York State Department of Transportation

political_signal.description: City leadership actively advancing STEAM High School infrastructure through street closure proposal and coordination with state agencies, indicating strong institutional commitment to education and facility development.

processing_notes: Article focuses on operational logistics rather than budgetary details
Street closure decision requires multi-agency coordination
